reflection.episodic_summary: Agent found a CT Abdomen procedure but accepted a 'CT Abdomen Pelvis' code as a match despite the instruction specifying only 'CT Abdomen'. The time check was accurate but the procedure type mismatch caused failure.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent matched a CT Abdomen Pelvis procedure as CT Abdomen, violating the rule that additional body part modifiers indicate a mismatch.
action_guidelines: [P1] When the requested procedure type is specific (e.g., 'CT Abdomen'), reject codes that include additional body parts (e.g., 'CT Abdomen Pelvis') as mismatches.
[P1] After identifying the most recent procedure, verify its exact code/description matches the requested type before proceeding with time-based logic.

kept: [P1] When a condition depends on a time interval (e.g., more than 12 months), first retrieve all relevant procedures without date filtering, then extract the most recent date for comparison.
[P1] When a procedure description includes an additional body part modifier (e.g., 'Pelvis'), treat it as a mismatch and search for the exact requested type.
[P1] Validate FHIR query parameters against known resource definitions before executing.
[P1] Validate any custom or non-standard codes against known terminologies (SNOMED, LOINC) before use.
[P1] When searching for medications, use standard coded identifiers (e.g., RxNorm, NDC) instead of display names.
[P1] For vaccination records, use Immunization resource instead of Procedure or MedicationRequest.
[P1] For time-window queries, specify both lower and upper bounds (ge and le) relative to current time to limit results to the window.
[P1] When a protocol requires multiple lab values, retrieve all specified labs before deciding on action.
[P1] Do not assume normal TSH means FT4 is unnecessary; follow the protocol exactly as stated.
[P1] Match FHIR resource type to clinical domain (e.g., ImagingStudy for radiology, Procedure for surgical actions, Device for indwelling devices) before querying.
[P1] If a query returns no results, consider alternative resource types or search parameters before concluding.

[P1] When a condition depends on absence of a prior event, confirm absence through exhaustive search (retry without code filter, without date filter, with alternative codes or display names) before taking action.
[P1] When searching for lab results, use patient ID combined with LOINC codes rather than relying on display names to avoid missing coded results.
[P1] Identify DVT prophylaxis by protocol-specific agents (e.g., heparin, enoxaparin, DOACs), not all anticoagulants.
[P1] When searching for devices, include status and date parameters to identify active devices; if no results, retry with broader parameters (e.g., no date filter) before concluding absence.
[P1] When a condition depends on a prior event (e.g., last dose >12 months), verify the event exists before taking action; absence of records does not automatically satisfy the condition.
[P1] When a lab result is missing, retry the search with alternative resource types (e.g., Observation vs. DiagnosticReport) and without date filters before concluding absence.
[P1] When checking time-based conditions, use the procedure or device insertion date as the reference point for calculating intervals, not the current time.
[P1] When initial vital sign search returns empty, retry without date filters or with broader category (vital-signs) before concluding absence.
[P1] If initial code-based search returns zero results, fall back to a patient+date search to identify the correct code or resource type.
[P1] When uncertain about codes, search by date range and patient ID without specifying a code.
[P1] Use the context-provided current time for ordering, not an arbitrary timestamp.
[P1] When searching for active orders, check the 'total' field in the search response and paginate through all pages before concluding absence.
[P1] If no results from a date-filtered query, retry the same search without the date parameter before concluding absence.
[P1] When verifying absence of active orders, search with status=active and no date filter to ensure completeness.

reflection.failure_analysis: Agent searched for active medication requests without specifying the naloxone code, resulting in an overly broad query that may not reliably identify naloxone orders.
action_guidelines: [P1] When verifying a matching prescription, search with the specific medication code (e.g., RxNorm or NDC) to avoid false matches.
[P1] After identifying an active opioid order, immediately search for a matching naloxone order using both patient ID and naloxone-specific code.
[P1] If uncertain about the correct code, first retrieve all active medication requests for the patient, then filter by code or description.

[P1] Validate any custom or non-standard codes against known terminologies (SNOMED, LOINC) before use.
[P1] When a procedure description includes an additional body part modifier (e.g., 'Pelvis'), treat it as a mismatch and search for the exact requested type.
[P1] When searching for medications, use standard coded identifiers (e.g., RxNorm, NDC) instead of display names.
[P1] When a protocol requires multiple lab values, retrieve all specified labs before deciding on action.
[P1] For time-window queries, specify both lower and upper bounds (ge and le) relative to current time to limit results to the window.
[P1] For vaccination records, use Immunization resource instead of Procedure or MedicationRequest.
[P1] Do not assume normal TSH means FT4 is unnecessary; follow the protocol exactly as stated.
[P1] Match FHIR resource type to clinical domain (e.g., ImagingStudy for radiology, Procedure for surgical actions, Device for indwelling devices) before querying.
[P1] If a query returns no results, consider alternative resource types or search parameters before concluding.
[P1] When searching for lab results, use patient ID combined with LOINC codes rather than relying on display names to avoid missing coded results.

[P1] When a condition depends on absence of a prior event, confirm absence through exhaustive search (retry without code filter, without date filter, with alternative codes or display names) before taking action.
[P1] Identify DVT prophylaxis by protocol-specific agents (e.g., heparin, enoxaparin, DOACs), not all anticoagulants.
[P1] When searching for devices, include status and date parameters to identify active devices; if no results, retry with broader parameters (e.g., no date filter) before concluding absence.
[P1] When a condition depends on a prior event (e.g., last dose >12 months), verify the event exists before taking action; absence of records does not automatically satisfy the condition.
[P1] When a lab result is missing, retry the search with alternative resource types (e.g., Observation vs. DiagnosticReport) and without date filters before concluding absence.
[P1] When checking time-based conditions, use the procedure or device insertion date as the reference point for calculating intervals, not the current time.
[P1] When initial vital sign search returns empty, retry without date filters or with broader category (vital-signs) before concluding absence.
[P1] If initial code-based search returns zero results, fall back to a patient+date search to identify the correct code or resource type.
[P1] When uncertain about codes, search by date range and patient ID without specifying a code.
[P1] Use the context-provided current time for ordering, not an arbitrary timestamp.
[P1] When searching for active orders, check the 'total' field in the search response and paginate through all pages before concluding absence.
[P1] If no results from a date-filtered query, retry the same search without the date parameter before concluding absence.
[P1] When verifying absence of active orders, search with status=active and no date filter to ensure completeness.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent searched for COVID-19 vaccine records in Immunization, MedicationRequest, and Procedure resources, found none, and incorrectly concluded that absence means last dose >12 months ago, ordering a booster without verifying patient vaccination status.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent failed by assuming missing records imply the condition is satisfied; absence of vaccine records does not mean the last dose was more than 12 months ago—it could mean the patient never received one or records are elsewhere.
action_guidelines: [P1] When a condition depends on a prior event (e.g., last dose >12 months), verify the event exists before taking action; absence of records does not automatically satisfy the condition.
[P1] For vaccination queries, search Immunization resource first; if no results, check for evidence of vaccination in other systems or patient history before concluding.
[P1] Do not order a medication based solely on absence of records; confirm the patient's eligibility criteria are met with documented evidence.
[P1] If no vaccine records found, stop and report the gap rather than proceeding with a treatment order.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent needed to check COVID-19 vaccination status and order booster if last dose >12 months ago. No vaccine records found. Agent incorrectly assumed absence means >12 months and ordered booster without verifying vaccination history.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent incorrectly concluded that absence of vaccination records automatically satisfies the 'more than 12 months ago' condition. The rule requires verifying the event exists before making time-based decisions; absence of records does not imply any time condition is met.
action_guidelines: [P1] When a condition depends on a prior event (e.g., last dose >12 months), verify the event exists before taking action; absence of records does not automatically satisfy the condition.
[P1] For vaccination status queries, search Immunization, MedicationRequest, and Procedure resources with date filters to find any existing records.
[P1] If no vaccination records are found, do not assume the time condition is met; instead, report that vaccination history cannot be confirmed.
[P1] Do not order medications based on unconfirmed assumptions about missing data; require explicit evidence of the triggering condition.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent found a single heparin order for DVT prophylaxis but did not verify its frequency matches the protocol (Q8H). It accepted Q12H without correction, violating the protocol requirement.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent failed because it did not enforce the protocol-specified dosing frequency (Q8H) for heparin DVT prophylaxis. It assumed any heparin order qualifies, missing the need to correct non-compliant orders.
action_guidelines: [P1] When verifying DVT prophylaxis, check that the order's dose and frequency exactly match the protocol (e.g., heparin 5000 units SC Q8H).
[P1] If an existing order deviates from protocol, treat it as non-compliant and take corrective action (e.g., discontinue and re-order correctly).
[P1] Do not accept a single active order as sufficient unless it meets all protocol parameters (agent, dose, route, frequency).

[P1] After identifying the order to keep, discontinue all others by setting status to 'stopped'.
[P1] If uncertain about the correct FHIR operation, review the API documentation before proceeding.
kept: [P1] When a protocol requires multiple lab values, retrieve all specified labs before deciding on action, and confirm absence of each lab independently before concluding no action.
[P1] For vital sign queries, search using standard LOINC or observation codes, not just display names.
[P1] Validate FHIR query parameters against known resource definitions before executing.
[P1] Validate any custom or non-standard codes against known terminologies (SNOMED, LOINC) before use.
[P1] When paginating, check the total field in the response to determine if further pages exist.
[P1] When searching for medications, use standard coded identifiers (e.g., RxNorm, NDC) instead of display names.

[P1] When a condition depends on absence of a prior event, confirm absence through exhaustive search (retry without code filter, without date filter, with alternative codes or display names, and across alternative resource types) before taking action.
[P1] Do not assume normal TSH means FT4 is unnecessary; follow the protocol exactly as stated.
[P1] Match FHIR resource type to clinical domain (e.g., ImagingStudy for radiology, Procedure for surgical actions, Device for indwelling devices) before querying.
[P1] For time-window queries, specify both lower and upper bounds (ge and le) relative to current time to limit results to the window.
[P1] If a query returns no results, consider alternative resource types or search parameters before concluding.
[P1] When searching for lab results, use patient ID combined with LOINC codes rather than relying on display names to avoid missing coded results.
[P1] When a condition depends on a time interval (e.g., more than 12 months), first retrieve all relevant procedures without date filtering, then extract the most recent date for comparison.
[P1] For vaccination records, use Immunization resource instead of Procedure or MedicationRequest.
[P1] When searching for devices, include status and date parameters to identify active devices; if no results, retry with broader parameters (e.g., no date filter) before concluding absence.
[P1] Identify DVT prophylaxis by protocol-specific agents (e.g., heparin, enoxaparin, DOACs), not all anticoagulants.
[P1] When a lab result is missing, retry the search with alternative resource types (e.g., Observation vs. DiagnosticReport) and without date filters before concluding absence.
[P1] When a procedure description includes an additional body part modifier (e.g., 'Pelvis'), treat it as a mismatch and search for the exact requested type.
[P1] When checking time-based conditions, use the procedure or device insertion date as the reference point for calculating intervals, not the current time.
[P1] If initial code-based search returns zero results, fall back to a patient+date search to identify the correct code or resource type.
[P1] When a condition depends on a prior event (e.g., last dose >12 months), verify the event exists before taking action; absence of records does not automatically satisfy the condition.
[P1] Use the context-provided current time for ordering, not an arbitrary timestamp.
[P1] When uncertain about codes, search by date range and patient ID without specifying a code.
[P1] If no results from a date-filtered query, retry the same search without the date parameter before concluding absence.
[P1] When verifying absence of active orders, search with status=active and no date filter to ensure completeness.
